perturbation.original_text: "Business Day" means any calendar day, other than a Saturday or Sunday, on which commercial banks in New Orleans, Louisiana are open for business.
perturbation.changed_text: "Business Day" means any calendar day, including Saturdays and Sundays, regardless of whether commercial banks in New Orleans, Louisiana are open for business.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of "Business Day" excludes Saturdays and Sundays. The modified definition includes them. This in-text contradiction creates uncertainty regarding deadlines and obligations tied to business days throughout the contract, specifically in Section 3.2 Owner Default, where failure to pay amounts due within a specified number of business days constitutes a breach, contradicting itself.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.2 Owner Default. Operator may terminate this Agreement at any time upon the occurrence of any of the following: (a) the dissolution or bankruptcy of Owner; (b) Owner fails to pay when due any amount due and payable hereunder, including, without limitation, any interest, fees, reimbursements or indemnifications, and such failure shall continue for a period of 15 days after the due date thereof; or (c) other than as set forth in clause (b) above, Owner breaches or fails to observe or perform any material term, condition, or obligation contained in this Agreement and fails to correct, or fails to diligently pursue correction of, such breach within 60 days after receipt of written notice from Operator of any such breach.
perturbation.justification: The changed definition of "Business Day" to include Saturdays and Sundays directly contradicts Section 3.2, which specifies a 15-day period for rectifying payment failures based on the original "Business Day" definition excluding weekends. This significantly alters the timeframe and creates a direct conflict regarding when payments are considered overdue and when termination can occur, as the 15-day period would now include weekend days that were previously excluded, making it a shorter actual timeframe to cure a breach.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1.4 Maintenance of the Rail Facility. Subject to the terms, conditions and limitations set forth in this Agreement, Owner hereby authorizes and empowers Operator, and Operator agrees, in the name of and on behalf of Owner, to, at its sole cost and expense, keep and maintain the Rail Facility in a condition and repair similar to, but not less than, its condition and repair on the Effective Date hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1.4 Maintenance of the Rail Facility. Subject to the terms, conditions and limitations set forth in this Agreement, Owner hereby authorizes and empowers Operator, and Operator agrees, in the name of and on behalf of Owner, to, at Owner's sole cost and expense, keep and maintain the Rail Facility in a condition and repair similar to, but not less than, its condition and repair on the Effective Date hereof.
perturbation.explanation: In the original clause, Operator is responsible for the cost of maintaining the Rail Facility. By changing "at its sole cost and expense" to "at Owner's sole cost and expense", we create an in-text contradiction. Now, two entities are claiming responsibility, causing uncertainty as to which party bears the financial burden for maintaining the rail facility. The contradiction creates an issue in Section 4.1.10 Payment of Operating Expenses.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1.10 Payment of Operating Expenses. Operator shall promptly pay all direct costs and expenses incurred in operating and maintaining the Rail Facility as they become due, without reimbursement by Owner save and except to the extent specifically provided otherwise herein.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the Operator is responsible for the cost of maintaining the Rail Facility. The changed text shifts this responsibility to the Owner. Section 4.1.10 explicitly states that 'Operator shall promptly pay all direct costs and expenses incurred in operating and maintaining the Rail Facility'. The changed text in 4.1.4, assigning maintenance costs to the Owner, directly contradicts this, creating a clear conflict and uncertainty about which party is financially responsible for maintenance.

perturbation.original_text: 8.13 Assignment. Operator shall not make any assignment of all or any part of this Agreement or any of the rights or obligations hereunder unless there first shall have been obtained the written consent thereto of Owner, which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld, conditioned, or delayed. Owner shall not make any assignment of all or any part of this Agreement or any of the rights or obligations hereunder except in connection with the sale, financing or conveyance of all or any part of the Rail Facility. This Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the successors and assigns of the Parties. Any attempted assignment of this Agreement in violation of this Section 8.12 shall be null and void.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.13 Assignment. Operator shall not make any assignment of all or any part of this Agreement or any of the rights or obligations hereunder unless there first shall have been obtained the written consent thereto of Owner, which consent may be withheld at Owner’s sole discretion, conditioned, or delayed. Owner shall not make any assignment of all or any part of this Agreement or any of the rights or obligations hereunder except in connection with the sale, financing or conveyance of all or any part of the Rail Facility. This Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the successors and assigns of the Parties. Any attempted assignment of this Agreement in violation of this Section 8.12 shall be null and void.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states that Owner's consent to Operator's assignment "shall not be unreasonably withheld, conditioned, or delayed." The modification changes this to "may be withheld at Owner’s sole discretion." This creates a direct contradiction, making it unclear under what conditions, if any, Operator can assign the agreement and creating uncertainty about the assignment clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.13 Assignment. Operator shall not make any assignment of all or any part of this Agreement or any of the rights or obligations hereunder unless there first shall have been obtained the written consent thereto of Owner, which consent may be withheld at Owner’s sole discretion, conditioned, or delayed. Owner shall not make any assignment of all or any part of this Agreement or any of the rights or obligations hereunder except in connection with the sale, financing or conveyance of all or any part of the Rail Facility. This Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the successors and assigns of the Parties. Any attempted assignment of this Agreement in violation of this Section 8.12 shall be null and void.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Owner's consent "shall not be unreasonably withheld". The changed text modifies this to allow the Owner to withhold consent at their "sole discretion." This creates a direct contradiction as the original text limits the Owner's ability to withhold consent, while the changed text gives the Owner the unrestricted right to do so. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, as the contradiction is created by the modification itself.
